source.cookbook: Black Rican Vegan   Fire Plant Based Recipes from
source.author: Various

ingredients.item:
COMPRA LIST
Moxtails
2 (14-oz [400-g]) cans jackfruit, drained and rinsed
2 lb (905 g) Beyond Meat ground beef
1 tsp salt
1 tsp chili powder
½ tsp red pepper flakes
1 tsp celery powder
1 tsp ground allspice
1 tsp garlic powder
1 tsp onion powder
1 tsp umami powder
2 to 3 (4.7-oz [134-g]) packages rice paper wrappers
Gravy
2 tbsp (30 ml) vegetable oil
2 tbsp (26 g) sugar
¼ cup (38 g) diced bell pepper, assorted colors
¼ cup (40 g) diced onion
3 cloves garlic, minced
2 tbsp (32 g) tomato paste
3 cups (710 ml) vegetable stock
1 tbsp (15 ml) soy sauce
1 tbsp (2.5 g) fresh thyme leaves
1 tsp ground allspice
½ tsp liquid smoke
1 Scotch bonnet pepper
¼ cup (60 ml) vegan barbecue sauce
1 (15.5-oz [439-g]) can butter beans, drained and rinsed

Serves 8
In New York City, this dish is highly requested at my pop-ups. Not many people make vegan moxtails because they are a process, but it’s so worth it in the end and it’s mind-blowing. The important part is mimicking the fat and shredded meat texture of actual oxtail. Oxtail is a popular dish made in Jamaica, and Hispanic cultures make them as well; we just call them rabo. You can now make these vegan and in the comfort of your own home, so you can save the feeling of anxiety of going to a Jamaican spot and getting grilled down by the lady making your plate. It’s okay to add more gravy, if you want!
COCINANDO
Make the Moxtails
Boil your jackfruit in a pot of water for 1 hour to extract the brine. After an hour, drain the jackfruit and squeeze out as much excess water as you can.
In a medium-sized bowl, combine the Beyond Meat ground beef and jackfruit, and season with the salt, chili powder, red pepper flakes, celery powder, allspice, garlic powder, onion powder and umami powder.
Set your station for assembly: Gather a large bowl of water and your rice paper wrappers. On your countertop or table, cover a large area with parchment paper or plastic wrap for rolling. Pick up two pieces of rice paper and submerge each into the bowl of water for 3 seconds. Place one flat on the plastic wrap, then layer the other on top of it, slightly to the side, to create a longer wrapper.
ingredients.original:
COMPRA LIST
Moxtails
2 (14-oz [400-g]) cans jackfruit, drained and rinsed
2 lb (905 g) Beyond Meat ground beef
1 tsp salt
1 tsp chili powder
½ tsp red pepper flakes
1 tsp celery powder
1 tsp ground allspice
1 tsp garlic powder
1 tsp onion powder
1 tsp umami powder
2 to 3 (4.7-oz [134-g]) packages rice paper wrappers
Gravy
2 tbsp (30 ml) vegetable oil
2 tbsp (26 g) sugar
¼ cup (38 g) diced bell pepper, assorted colors
¼ cup (40 g) diced onion
3 cloves garlic, minced
2 tbsp (32 g) tomato paste
3 cups (710 ml) vegetable stock
1 tbsp (15 ml) soy sauce
1 tbsp (2.5 g) fresh thyme leaves
1 tsp ground allspice
½ tsp liquid smoke
1 Scotch bonnet pepper
¼ cup (60 ml) vegan barbecue sauce
1 (15.5-oz [439-g]) can butter beans, drained and rinsed

Serves 8
In New York City, this dish is highly requested at my pop-ups. Not many people make vegan moxtails because they are a process, but it’s so worth it in the end and it’s mind-blowing. The important part is mimicking the fat and shredded meat texture of actual oxtail. Oxtail is a popular dish made in Jamaica, and Hispanic cultures make them as well; we just call them rabo. You can now make these vegan and in the comfort of your own home, so you can save the feeling of anxiety of going to a Jamaican spot and getting grilled down by the lady making your plate. It’s okay to add more gravy, if you want!
COCINANDO
Make the Moxtails
Boil your jackfruit in a pot of water for 1 hour to extract the brine. After an hour, drain the jackfruit and squeeze out as much excess water as you can.
In a medium-sized bowl, combine the Beyond Meat ground beef and jackfruit, and season with the salt, chili powder, red pepper flakes, celery powder, allspice, garlic powder, onion powder and umami powder.
Set your station for assembly: Gather a large bowl of water and your rice paper wrappers. On your countertop or table, cover a large area with parchment paper or plastic wrap for rolling. Pick up two pieces of rice paper and submerge each into the bowl of water for 3 seconds. Place one flat on the plastic wrap, then layer the other on top of it, slightly to the side, to create a longer wrapper.

ingredients.item: Place about 1 cup (329 g) of the moxtail mixture in the center of the layered rice paper. Fold the long, vertical sides of the rice paper over the moxtail mixture. Then, from the unfolded horizontal side, begin to wrap and tuck the moxtail mixture into the rice paper. Roll it up; from the side, you should see swirls of rice paper in between the moxtail. This will create the fatty part of the moxtail and hold the meat together. Wrap the moxtail roll with plastic wrap. Repeat with all of your rice papers and the moxtail mixture, then place them in the freezer for 2 hours or overnight.

Then, from the unfolded horizontal side, begin to wrap and tuck the moxtail mixture into the rice paper. Roll it up; from the side, you should see swirls of rice paper in between the moxtail. This will create the fatty part of the moxtail and hold the meat together. Wrap the moxtail roll with plastic wrap. Repeat with all of your rice papers and the moxtail mixture, then place them in the freezer for 2 hours or overnight.
